title: Noumenal journal manifest (noumenal.json, version 2 — units)
description: The Verified ML Journal structure. The citable atom is a (statement, proof, witness) triple. A unit is ACCEPTED iff the three-check gate passes: PROOF (kernel) AND FAITHFULNESS (certified gloss) AND WITNESS (executed value inside a kernel-certified interval). Tier (T0/T1/T2) is DERIVED, never declared. SUBSTRATE EXCEPTION: the proof leg and the witness leg carry TWO INDEPENDENT dependency closures, each terminating in its own named trust base; there is no shared-substrate edge joining experiment to theory. v1 card manifests still render; v2 is the authoring contract the staging skill emits and CI re-verifies.

unit.description: One self-contained (statement, proof, witness) triple. NOTE: there is intentionally NO unit-level `substrate` field; proof.closure and witness.closure are independent (the substrate exception).

tier.description: DERIVED by the skill, RE-DERIVED authoritatively by CI. T0 = kernel-checked proof, axiom-audited. T1 = + an executable witness that runs. T2 = + the executed value lands in the certified interval on held-out inputs.

superseded_by.description: DOI of the unit that supersedes this one (when status = superseded-by)

proof.description: Leg 1 (kernel). The theory leg, with its OWN closure + base.

code.description: the declaration text (public repos: pulled + validated live)

sorry_free.description: false => this is an open ChallengeCrown, not an accepted unit

base.description: the theory leg's named trust base

closure.description: theory dependency closure: DOIs this proof imports. INDEPENDENT of witness.closure.

witness.description: Leg 3 (sandbox). The experiment leg, with its OWN closure + base. kind=none => tier T0 (no witness).

binding.description: in-repo path to the executed-binding / experiment artifact

interval_decl.description: the interval-membership theorem name (kernel-checked, not asserted)

interval.description: the certified interval [lo, hi] the executed value must land in

sandbox.description: how the witness runs (and inside what bounds)

base.description: the EXPERIMENT leg's named trust base — SEPARATE from proof.base

closure.description: experiment dependency closure: DOIs the witness imports. INDEPENDENT of proof.closure; no shared-substrate edge.

gloss.description: Leg 2 (faithfulness). Author drafts, editor certifies. No gloss, no DOI.

text.description: the reviewed natural-language gloss: what the theorem means

certified_by.description: editor handle once faithfulness is certified; null until then

checks.description: The three-check gate. Authoritative values are written by CI re-verification; the skill fills in its local pre-check.

accepted.description: proof==pass AND faithfulness==pass AND (tier==T0 ? true : witness==pass)

doi.description: minted on Zenodo deposit (the immutable snapshot: pinned toolchain + lockfile)

base.description: A NAMED trust base — 'verified modulo a named base, not from nothing'.

axioms.description: whitelist actually used (subset of {propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound} for the kernel-clean base)

runtime.description: for the witness base: the execution runtime / sandbox image
